selected_important_turn.text: Hello, i will offer you as much food as you want for all of your fire wood.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the opening move that frames the negotiation as zero-sum and extractive by asking for all of the opponent's firewood in exchange for unlimited food. Early framing strongly influences how the other party interprets motives and what concessions they'll expect later.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is overly aggressive (requests 'all' firewood) and was made before eliciting any preferences. That locks the interaction into a competitive frame, risks undermining trust and willingness to share information, and may foreclose integrative trades that raise joint value.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Start by asking about the other side's priorities or propose a tentative, flexible package rather than demanding all of one resource. Signal openness to tradeoffs (e.g., ask what they value most and offer a few potential trades) instead of making an all-or-nothing claim.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: Currently likely reduced because the move narrows possible tradeoffs; changing it to an information-seeking or flexible opening would likely increase joint gains by enabling integrative options.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: Perceived fairness is lowered by an extractive opening; a softer, information-seeking opening would increase perceived fairness.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: Harder now because the opponent may respond defensively; a more cooperative opening would make agreement easier and faster.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: This aggressive start risks damaging rapport; a friendlier, curiosity-driven opening would improve relationship and trust.

value2reason.Medium: I am very thirsty but not dying yet.
value2reason.High: I haven't eaten in three days.

value2reason.Low: I already have 4 cases of 48 bottles of water - sufficient for everyone to have 10 bottles.
value2reason.Medium: My entire family is coming on the camping trip and I don't have enough to feed them all, else my kids will only be eating marshmallows.
value2reason.High: I don't have any means to cook my food, which is predominantly raw meat.  The forecast is also going to be cold weather and I need a source of heat.

dialogue.text: Hello, i will offer you as much food as you want for all of your fire wood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need firewood for cooking and warmth. I'll trade 3 firewood for 2 food packages. That gives me heat and helps feed my family. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand the importance of firewood for cooking and warmth. Since I value food highly, I'm willing to trade 2 food packages for 1 firewood package. What do you think about this offer?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade 2 food packages for 1 firewood package. It aligns with my priority for heat and family meals. Let's finalize this deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
